title: regent fix output (v1)
description: Wire format of `regent fix --format json` (issue #62). The document carries exactly three top-level keys: edits the engine applied to disk (`applied`), edits the engine surfaced for the agent to judge (`suggested`), and edits the engine deferred because it could not resolve them (`deferred`). Top-level additional properties are forbidden — additions go into v2.

applied.description: Edits the engine wrote to disk. Safe-lane deterministic fixes only.

suggested.description: Edits the engine did NOT apply (safety='suggested' or guidance-only). The agent decides per item.

deferred.description: Edits the engine could not resolve — overlapping edits, out-of-range offsets, or rules with no fix attached.

proposedEdit.description: Concrete byte-span replacement the agent can choose to apply.

appliedEdit.description: Edit the engine applied. `before` and `after` are the exact substrings (UTF-16 code units) that were replaced.

suggestedEdit.description: Edit the engine surfaced for agent judgement. `proposedEdit` is null for guidance-only fixes; otherwise it is the byte-span replacement the rule computed.

context.description: Lines around the match (the ±contextBuffer from the runner). Each entry is the raw line text without trailing newline.

deferredEdit.description: Edit the engine could not apply. `reason` is a stable token; the human-readable detail (e.g. the winning ruleId on overlap) is appended after a colon-and-space.

reason.description: `overlap with <winning-ruleId>` when a higher-priority edit won the byte span; `out-of-range` when the file changed mid-run; `no-fix-attached` when the rule fired without a `fix` attachment.
